description: This transmission electron micrograph displays the dislocation substructure in an Fe–15 at.% Al alloy deformed to 2–3% compression strain at room temperature and a strain rate of 3×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹. The image reveals single dislocations with Burgers vector (1/2)<111> arranged in a loose cellular distribution, characteristic of disordered or short-range ordered bcc materials at low plastic strain. The micrograph was acquired with the zone axis (stress axis) near [0 1 3] using the g = 200 reflection, as indicated by the arrow. Visible features include smoothly curved dislocation segments forming incipient cell boundaries where dislocations show weak clustering. Many segments in these clustered regions appear as dipoles, consistent with dislocation debris trailing from gliding screw dislocations during deformation. The scale bar represents 0.25 μm. This microstructure illustrates the early stages of work hardening in low-Al Fe–Al alloys, where individual dislocations dominate plastic deformation, contrasting with the superdislocation pairs observed in higher-Al ordered compositions.
